person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
geni.com
Eleanor d'Aquitaine, Queen Consort Of England
French: Aliénor d'Aquitaine, Queen Consort Of England, Lithuanian: Eleonora Akvitanietė, Queen Consort Of England, Spanish: Leonor de Aquitania, Queen Consort Of England
Also Known As:	"Princess Eleanor of Aquitaine", "Queen Consort of England and France", "Aliénor d’Aquitaine", "Éléonore de Guyenne", "Countesse of Poitiers et Duchesse of Aquitaine", "Eleanor of Aquitaine", "Elbeonore Of /Aquitaine/", "Eleanora", "Que...", "Duchess of Aquitaine", "Queen ..."
Birthdate:	1122
Birthplace:	Nantes, Loire-Atlantique, Pays de la Loire, France
Death:	April 01, 1204 (81-82)
Poitiers, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, France 
Place of Burial:	Fontevraud-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France 
Immediate Family:	
Daughter of William X, Duke of Aquitaine and Eleanor of Châtellerault, Duchess of Aquitaine

Wife of Louis VII the Young, king of France and Henry II "Curtmantle", king of England

Mother of 
Marie Capet de France, comtesse de Champagne; 
Alice de France, Comtesse de Blois; 
William IX, count of Poitiers; 
Henry the Young King; 
Geoffrey II, duke of Brittany; 
Philip, Prince of England; 
Eleanor of England, Queen consort of Castile; 
Joan Plantagenet of England, Queen of Sicily;
 John I "Lackland", King of England; 
Matilda of England, Duchess of Saxony and
 Richard "the Lionheart", king of England 

Sister of Petronilla d'Aquitaine and Guillaume d'Aquitaine

Occupation:	One of the wealthiest and most powerful women in Western Europe during the High Middle Ages. As well as being Duchess of Aquitaine in her own right, she was queen consort of France 1137–1152 and queen consort of England 1154–1189., Queen, Monarch


Daughter of Guillaume X Duke of Aquitaine, and Aenor de Châtellerault.   Duchess Eleanor Aquitaine was the Countess of Poitiers and Queen of England

Eleanor of Aquitaine (1122 – 1 April 1204) (French: Aliénor d'Aquitaine) was queen consort of France (1137–1152)[1] and England (1154–1189) and duchess of Aquitaine in her own right (1137–1204). As the heir of the House of Poitiers, rulers in southwestern France, she was one of the wealthiest and most powerful women in western Europe during the High Middle Ages. She was patron of literary figures such as Wace, Benoît de Sainte-Maure, and Bernart de Ventadorn. She led armies several times in her life and was a leader of the Second Crusade.

As the duchess of Aquitaine, Eleanor was the most eligible bride in Europe. Three months after becoming duchess upon the death of her father, William X, she married King Louis VII of France, son of her guardian, King Louis VI. As queen of France, she participated in the unsuccessful Second Crusade. Soon afterwards, Eleanor sought an annulment of her marriage,[2] but her request was rejected by Pope Eugene III.[3] However, after the birth of her second daughter Alix, Louis agreed to an annulment, as 15 years of marriage had not produced a son.[4] The marriage was annulled on 21 March 1152 on the grounds of consanguinity within the fourth degree. Their daughters were declared legitimate, custody was awarded to Louis, and Eleanor's lands were restored to her.

As soon as the annulment was granted, Eleanor became engaged to the Duke of Normandy, who became King Henry II of England in 1154. Henry was her third cousin and 11 years younger. The couple married on Whitsun, 18 May 1152, eight weeks after the annulment of Eleanor's first marriage, in Poitiers Cathedral. Over the next 13 years, she bore eight children: five sons, three of whom became kings; and three daughters. However, Henry and Eleanor eventually became estranged. Henry imprisoned her in 1173 for supporting their son Henry's revolt against him. She was not released until 6 July 1189, when her husband Henry died and their third son, Richard the Lionheart, ascended the throne.

As queen dowager, Eleanor acted as regent while Richard went on the Third Crusade. Eleanor also lived well into the reign of Richard's heir and her youngest son, John.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre於1122年12月6日出生在法國波特-哈倫斯維安波爾斯時，她的父親Guillaume X d'Aquitaine Duc d'Aquitaine23歲，母親Eléonore de Châtellerault20歲。她於1137年7月25日與Louis VII le Jeune roi de France在法國阿奎泰吉洛德波迪奧結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於1204年3月31日在法國米迪-列內斯塔-吉加洛享年81歲，埋葬在法國安喬豐特福勞爾吉利阿巴易。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre于1122年12月6日出生在法国波特-哈倫斯維安波爾斯时，她的父亲Guillaume X d'Aquitaine Duc d'Aquitaine23岁，母亲Eléonore de Châtellerault20岁。她于1137年7月25日与Louis VII le Jeune roi de France在法国阿奎泰吉洛德波迪奧结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。她去世于1204年3月31日在法国米迪-列內斯塔-吉加洛享年81岁，埋葬在法国安喬豐特福勞爾吉利阿巴易。

en.lifeSummary: When Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre was born on 6 December 1122, in Poitiers, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, France, her father, Guillaume X d'Aquitaine Duc d'Aquitaine, was 23 and her mother, Eléonore de Châtellerault, was 20. She married Louis VII le Jeune roi de France on 25 July 1137, in Bordeaux, Gironde, Aquitaine, France. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died on 31 March 1204, in Tarn-et-Garonne, Midi-Pyrénées, France, at the age of 81, and was buried in Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre est née le 6 décembre 1122, à Poitiers, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, France, son père, Guillaume X d'Aquitaine Duc d'Aquitaine, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Eléonore de Châtellerault, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Louis VII le Jeune roi de France le 25 juillet 1137, à Bordeaux, Gironde, Aquitaine, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 31 mars 1204, à Tarn-et-Garonne, Midi-Pyrénées, France, à l’âge de 81 ans, et a été enterrée à Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre am 6. Dezember 1122 in Poitiers, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Guillaume X d'Aquitaine Duc d'Aquitaine 23 und ihre Mutter Eléonore de Châtellerault 20. Sie hat am 25. Juli 1137 in Bordeaux, Gironde, Aquitaine, Frankreich Louis VII le Jeune roi de France geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 31. März 1204 im Alter von 81 Jahren in Tarn-et-Garonne, Midi-Pyrénées, Frankreich und wurde in Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre è nata il il 6 dicembre 1122 nel Poitiers, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, Francia, sua padre, Guillaume X d'Aquitaine Duc d'Aquitaine, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Eléonore de Châtellerault, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Louis VII le Jeune roi de France il 25 luglio 1137, nel Bordeaux, Gironde, Aquitaine, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì il 31 marzo 1204, nel Tarn-et-Garonne, Midi-Pyrénées, Francia, all'età di 81 anni, e fu sepolta a Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterreが1122年12月6日にフランスポイトウ-チャーエンツヴィエンポイティエルズで生まれた時，父Guillaume X d'Aquitaine Duc d'Aquitaineは23歳，母Eléonore de Châtelleraultは20歳であった。1137年7月25日にフランスアクイティアインギロンドボルディーウックスでLouis VII le Jeune roi de Franceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1204年3月31日にフランスミディ-プイルeンeスターン-エト-ガーオンで81歳で死去し，フランスアンジョウフォンテvラウルト-ル'アッバイにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre(이)가 1122년 12월 6일 프랑스 포이토우-처츠 비니 포이티어스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Guillaume X d'Aquitaine Duc d'Aquitaine(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eléonore de Châtellerault(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1137년 7월 25일 프랑스 아키테인 지론드 볼도스에서 Louis VII le Jeune roi de France(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1204년 3월 31일 프랑스 미디-p이ee스 터아른-트-가니에서 81세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 앤조우 폰테브라트-'압베이에 묻혔다.

Ela faleceu em 31 de março de 1204, em Tarn-et-Garonne, Midi-Pyrénées, França, com 81 anos, e foi sepultada em Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre родилась 6 декабря 1122 г. в местности поитиирс, виенн, поитоу-чарентесь, Франция, её отцу по имени Guillaume X d'Aquitaine Duc d'Aquitaine было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Eléonore de Châtellerault, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Louis VII le Jeune roi de France 25 июля 1137 г. в местности бордокс, гиронд, аквитеин, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла 31 марта 1204 г. в местности тарн-ет-гаронн, миди-пирeнeесь, Франция в возрасте 81 год и была похоронена в местности фонтевраулт-л'аббеи, анджоу, Франция.
